market_positions.side: No - Los Angeles Lakers over 104.5 points

first_quarter.read: OKC established early defensive pressure and rim deterrence while LAL remained within range through Rui shot-making and late-quarter stabilization.
first_quarter.model_signal: Initial Lakers offensive suppression was present but not decisive enough for a live under without a large cushion.

second_quarter.read: LAL won the quarter 35-30, shot 65% from the field, and carried a 58-57 halftime lead. This created the halftime strike context but also exposed that Lakers role scoring was hotter than expected.
second_quarter.model_signal: Halftime under entry should have been downgraded because LAL had already reached 58 on efficient shooting, with Reaves/Smart/Kennard all contributing.

third_quarter.read: OKC flipped the game with a 36-22 quarter. Lakers committed 7 turnovers in the period and allowed OKC to generate 9 second-chance points and 6 fast-break points.
third_quarter.model_signal: This phase validated the OKC pressure script but created a dangerous late-game starter-extension zone, not a clean under zone.

fourth_quarter.read: LAL still scored 27 in the fourth despite trailing by double digits. Reaves and LeBron remained productive before bench minutes added late points.
fourth_quarter.model_signal: Playoff under failed because late-game extension plus free throws and deep-bench points broke suppression assumptions.

pivotal_moments.note: LiveFlow entered LAL under 104.5 after a hot LAL first half. The entry had insufficient buffer because LAL needed only 47 second-half points to beat the number.
SGA flagrant sequence and Caruso technical created a noisy high-leverage possession, but OKC later stabilized the quarter.
Jaylin Williams and Jared McCain threes pushed OKC from 81-74 to 89-76, creating the double-digit lead regime.
LeBron and Reaves hit back-to-back threes/attacks to keep LAL starters active and extend the scoring environment.

Late substitutions did not suppress scoring enough. Nick Smith Jr., Dalton Knecht, Nikola Topic, and Jared McCain produced late points that lifted the final total and confirmed garbage-time volatility.

model_relevance_summary.primary_failure: LAL had already reached 58 at halftime, leaving too little cushion for a 104.5 under in a playoff environment with starter extension and free-throw paths still live.
model_relevance_summary.secondary_failure: OKC defensive pressure produced turnovers but also created extra possessions, free throws, and transition/late-game scoring, keeping the game from becoming a clean under profile.
confirmed_signals: LAL turnover vulnerability without Luka Doncic
Chet Holmgren rim/defensive activity as a stabilizer
OKC bench scoring depth through Jared McCain/Cason Wallace
failed_assumptions: Double-digit playoff lead would suppress Lakers team total pace enough after halftime
Lakers scoring would regress sharply after hot second quarter
Bench-time scoring would be neutral or under-friendly
forward_model_notes: Require at least 3.5-point buffer below team-total under line for playoff LiveFlow entries.
Do not auto-fire under when the target team already has 55+ at halftime unless pace/FTA/shot quality are all collapsing.
Apply playoff starter-extension gate when spread is 8-18 entering 4Q; stars often stay in long enough to kill unders.
Add garbage-time bench scoring risk when OKC depth units include McCain, Wallace, Joe, or Topic with green-light 3PA.

recommended_patch_queue.rule: No playoff team-total under unless model median is at least 3.5 points below the live line.
If target team has 55+ points at halftime, require clear pace decline plus FTA suppression before under entry.
For playoff spreads between 8 and 18 entering Q4, assume primary scorers remain active until under 4:00 unless lead reaches 20+.
When OKC bench scorers are active late, downgrade full-game and team-total unders because bench 3PA remains aggressive.

rolling_window_use: High-weight Game 3 input for Lakers turnover/scoring volatility and OKC second-half pressure. Low-weight for clean under projections because late-game and bench scoring distorted the suppression profile.
